title: Meet the Terminal
description: Say hello to your new superpower: the command line! It might look scary, but it's your best friend.

steps.instruction: Every Linux pro knows this shortcut: press Ctrl+Alt+T to open the Terminal instantly!
steps.hint: Hold Ctrl and Alt, then press T. This is the most famous Linux shortcut!

steps.instruction: The terminal is ready! Type: pwd — This stands for 'Print Working Directory' and shows where you are.
steps.hint: Type 'pwd' and press Enter to see your current location in the filesystem.

steps.instruction: You should see /home/you — that's your home directory! Now type: ls — to see what's in here.
steps.hint: Type 'ls' and press Enter to list all files and folders.

steps.instruction: You can see Desktop, Documents, Downloads, etc. Type: cd Documents — to move into the Documents folder.
steps.hint: Type 'cd Documents' and press Enter to change directory.

steps.instruction: Now type: ls — to see what's in Documents. You should find some fun text files!
Type: cd ~ — The tilde (~) is a shortcut for your home directory. It takes you home instantly!
steps.hint: Type 'cd ~' and press Enter to go back to your home directory.
